add_component_to_blueprint.description: Add a component to a Blueprint.

add_event_node.description:
Add an event node to a Blueprint graph.

    Create specialized event nodes (ReceiveBeginPlay, ReceiveTick, etc.)
    in a Blueprint's event graph at specified positions.

    Args:
        bluep

add_event_override.description:
Override a parent class event/function in a Blueprint (e.g. OnRebuildGeneratedMesh, ReceiveBeginPlay).
    Creates the function override graph and returns the entry node ID and its output pins.
    Us

add_function_input.description:
Add an input parameter to a Blueprint function.

    Args:
        blueprint_name: Name of the Blueprint to modify
        function_name: Name of the function
        param_name: Name of the inpu

add_function_output.description:
Add an output parameter to a Blueprint function.

    Args:
        blueprint_name: Name of the Blueprint to modify
        function_name: Name of the function
        param_name: Name of the out

add_node.description:
Add a node to a Blueprint graph.

    Create various types of K2Nodes in a Blueprint's event graph or function graph.
    Supports 23 node types organized by category.

    Args:
        blueprin

analyze_blueprint_graph.description:
Analyze a specific graph within a Blueprint (EventGraph, functions, etc.)
    and provide detailed information about nodes, connections, and execution flow.

apply_material_to_actor.description: Apply a specific material to an actor in the level.

apply_material_to_blueprint.description: Apply a specific material to a component in a Blueprint.

asset_delete.description: Delete an asset. Use with caution.

asset_duplicate.description: Duplicate an asset to a new path.

asset_exists.description: Check if an asset exists at the given path.

asset_find.description: Search for assets by name. Returns matching assets with paths.

asset_get_info.description: Get detailed info about an asset: class, package, references, is dirty.

asset_list.description:
List assets in a directory. Returns path, name, and class for each.
        class_filter: optional, e.g. 'Material', 'StaticMesh', 'Blueprint'

batch_execute.description:
Execute multiple MCP commands in a single network round-trip.
        commands: list of {"type": "command_name", "params": {...}} objects.
        Example: [{"type": "gs_create_mesh", "params": {"mesh

connect_nodes.description:
Connect two nodes in a Blueprint graph.

    Links a source pin to a target pin between existing nodes in a Blueprint's event graph or function graph.

    Args:
        blueprint_name: Name of t

construct_house.description: Construct a realistic house with architectural details and multiple rooms.

construct_mansion.description:
Construct a magnificent mansion with multiple wings, grand rooms, gardens,
    fountains, and luxury features perfect for dramatic TikTok reveals.

create_aqueduct.description:
Build a multi-tier Roman-style aqueduct with arches and water channel.
    
    Creates a majestic aqueduct with repeating arches, support piers, and
    a water channel deck. Each tier has progr

create_arch.description: Create a simple arch using cubes in a semicircle.

create_castle_fortress.description:
Create a massive castle fortress with walls, towers, courtyards, throne room,
    and surrounding village. Perfect for dramatic TikTok reveals showing
    the scale and detail of a complete medie

create_function.description:
Create a new function in a Blueprint.

    Args:
        blueprint_name: Name of the Blueprint to modify
        function_name: Name for the new function
        return_type: Return type of the f

create_maze.description: Create a proper solvable maze with entrance, exit, and guaranteed path using recursive backtracking algorithm.

create_pyramid.description: Spawn a pyramid made of cube actors.

create_suspension_bridge.description:
Build a suspension bridge with towers, deck, cables, and suspenders.
    
    Creates a realistic suspension bridge with parabolic main cables, vertical
    suspenders, twin towers, and a multi-l

create_tower.description: Create a realistic tower with various architectural styles.

create_town.description: Create a full dynamic town with buildings, streets, infrastructure, and vehicles.

create_variable.description:
Create a variable in a Blueprint.

    Adds a new variable to a Blueprint with specified type, default value, and properties.

    Args:
        blueprint_name: Name of the Blueprint to modify

create_wall.description: Create a simple wall from cubes.

delete_function.description:
Delete a function from a Blueprint.

    Args:
        blueprint_name: Name of the Blueprint to modify
        function_name: Name of the function to delete

    Returns:
        Dictionary with

delete_node.description:
Delete a node from a Blueprint graph.

    Removes a node and all its connections from either the EventGraph
    or a specific function graph.

    Args:
        blueprint_name: Name of the Bluep

execute_python_file_in_editor.description:
Execute a Python script file inside the Unreal Editor.
        file_path: absolute path to the .py file on disk.
        The file is read and executed in the editor's Python environment.

execute_python_in_editor.description:
Execute Python code directly inside the Unreal Editor.
        The code runs in the editor's Python environment with full access to unreal module.
        Example: 'import unreal; print(unreal.EditorA

get_actor_material_info.description: Get information about the materials currently applied to an actor.

get_actors_in_level.description: Get a list of all actors in the current level.

get_available_materials.description: Get a list of available materials in the project that can be applied to objects.

get_blueprint_function_details.description:
Get detailed information about Blueprint functions including parameters,
    return values, local variables, and function graph content.
    
    Args:
        blueprint_path: Full path to the Bl

get_blueprint_variable_details.description:
Get detailed information about Blueprint variables including type, 
    default values, metadata, and usage within the Blueprint.
    
    Args:
        blueprint_path: Full path to the Blueprint

gs_append_box.description:
Append a box primitive to a named dynamic mesh.
        Creates the mesh if it doesn't exist. Steps add subdivisions.

gs_append_capsule.description: Append a capsule (cylinder with hemisphere caps) to a named dynamic mesh.

gs_append_cone.description: Append a cone (or truncated cone) primitive. Set top_radius > 0 for frustum.

gs_append_cylinder.description: Append a cylinder primitive to a named dynamic mesh.

gs_append_mesh.description:
Append (combine) one mesh into another with an optional transform.
        Unlike boolean union, this simply merges triangles without resolving intersections.
        Much faster than boolean union wh

gs_append_revolve.description:
Revolve a 2D profile path around the Y axis to create a surface of revolution.
        path_points: list of [x, y] coordinates defining the profile (min 2 points).
        Use revolve_degrees < 360 fo

gs_append_sphere.description: Append a sphere primitive to a named dynamic mesh.

gs_append_sweep.description:
Sweep a 2D polygon along a 3D path to create extruded geometry.
        polygon_vertices: list of [x, y] defining the cross-section (min 3 vertices).
        sweep_path: list of {"location": [x, y, z]

gs_append_torus.description: Append a torus (donut shape) primitive to a named dynamic mesh.

gs_auto_uvs.description:
Auto-generate patch-based UVs for a mesh.
        Best general-purpose UV unwrapping for procedural geometry.

gs_bend.description:
Apply a bend warp deformation to a mesh. Bends the mesh along its Z axis.
        angle: bend angle in degrees. bounds: Z range affected.

gs_bevel.description:
Bevel all polygroup edges on a mesh. Rounds sharp edges.
        subdivisions: extra edge loops for smoother rounding.
        round_weight: 0=flat, 1=round.

gs_boolean.description:
Apply a boolean operation between two named meshes.
        operation: 'union', 'intersect', or 'subtract' (default).
        output_mesh: name for result (defaults to target_mesh, overwriting it).

gs_box_projection.description:
Project UVs using a box projection. Good for boxy shapes.
        Projects from 6 cube faces, each face picks the closest projection.

gs_create_mesh.description:
Create an empty named dynamic mesh in the Geometry Script registry.
        This mesh can then be modified with append, boolean, and deformation operations.
        Must be called before other gs_* op

gs_flare.description:
Apply a flare (taper) warp deformation. Scales X/Y based on Z position.
        flare_x/y: percentage of scaling at the top of the bounds.

gs_get_bounds.description: Get detailed bounding box: min, max, center, and extent for each axis.

gs_inset_outset_faces.description:
Inset (positive distance) or outset (negative) faces within their plane.
        Creates a border ring around selected faces. Great for adding detail.
        material_id: only inset faces with this m

gs_is_point_inside.description: Test if a point is inside a closed mesh volume. Uses BVH for fast queries.

gs_linear_extrude_faces.description:
Extrude faces of a mesh linearly along a direction.
        direction: [x,y,z] fixed direction. If omitted, extrudes along average face normals.
        material_id: only extrude faces with this mater

gs_list_meshes.description: List all dynamic meshes currently in the registry with their vertex/triangle counts.

gs_offset.description:
Offset mesh surface uniformly by a distance (inflate/deflate).
        Positive distance inflates, negative deflates.

gs_offset_faces.description:
Offset selected faces along their normals (push in/out).
        material_id: only offset faces with this material ID. If omitted, offsets all faces.

gs_perlin_noise.description:
Apply Perlin noise displacement to a mesh surface.
        Creates organic-looking surface variation. Great for terrain patches.

gs_planar_projection.description:
Project UVs from a plane onto the mesh. Good for flat surfaces.
        The plane orientation is defined by location and rotation.

gs_query_mesh_info.description: Get basic mesh info: vertex count, triangle count, and bounding box.

gs_query_vertices.description: Get vertex positions from a mesh. Returns up to max_vertices positions.

gs_raycast.description:
Cast a ray against a mesh using BVH acceleration.
        Returns hit position, triangle ID, and ray parameter if hit.

gs_release_mesh.description: Release a named dynamic mesh from the registry, freeing its memory.

gs_select_all.description: Select all triangles in a mesh. Returns the count of selected elements.

gs_select_by_material.description: Select all triangles with a specific material ID.

gs_select_by_normal.description:
Select triangles whose normal is within max_angle degrees of the given direction.
        normal=[0,0,1] with max_angle=30 selects upward-facing faces.

gs_select_in_box.description: Select triangles inside a bounding box.

gs_self_union.description:
Apply self-union to resolve self-intersections in a mesh.
        Useful after combining multiple primitives that overlap.

gs_shell.description: Create a hollow shell from a solid mesh with the given wall thickness.

gs_smooth.description:
Apply iterative Laplacian smoothing to a mesh.
        Reduces sharp edges and noise. More iterations = smoother but loses detail.

gs_twist.description:
Apply a twist warp deformation around the Z axis.
        angle: total twist angle in degrees across the bounds range.

inspect_class.description:
List all BlueprintCallable functions and visible properties of any UE class.
        class_name: e.g. 'Actor', 'StaticMeshComponent', 'GeneratedDynamicMeshActor'
        include_inherited: include par

inspect_enum.description:
List all values of a UEnum type.
        enum_name: e.g. 'EBlendMode', 'EGeometryScriptBooleanOperation'
        Use this to discover valid parameter values for enum-typed pins.

level_focus_actor.description: Focus the viewport camera on a specific actor.

level_get_actor_properties.description: Get all properties of an actor: class, transform, components, visible UPROPERTYs.

level_get_component_properties.description: Get properties of a specific component on an actor.

level_get_info.description: Get level info: name, actor count by class, world bounds.

level_screenshot.description: Capture viewport screenshot to a PNG file. Returns the file path.

level_set_actor_property.description: Set a property on an actor. Supports location/rotation/scale as [x,y,z], bool, int, float, string.

level_set_camera.description: Set the editor viewport camera position and rotation.

level_spawn_blueprint.description:
Spawn a Blueprint actor in the level by its asset path.
        blueprint_path: e.g. '/Game/Blueprints/BP_GS_Cup'

mat_add_expression.description:
Add a material expression node. Returns node_id for connections.
        expression_class: MaterialExpressionConstant, MaterialExpressionConstant3Vector,
        MaterialExpressionFresnel, MaterialExp

mat_apply_to_actor.description: Apply a material to an actor's mesh component.

mat_connect.description:
Connect output of one expression to input of another.
        source_node_id: ID of the source node. target_node_id: ID of the target node.
        input_pin_name: name of the input pin (use mat_list_

mat_connect_to_output.description:
Connect an expression to a material output property.
        material_property: BaseColor, Roughness, Metallic, Specular, Normal,
        Opacity, Refraction, EmissiveColor, FrontMaterial (for Substra

mat_create_material.description:
Create a new material asset.
        blend_mode: Opaque, Translucent, TranslucentColoredTransmittance, TranslucentGreyTransmittance, Additive, Modulate
        shading_model: DefaultLit, Unlit, ThinTr

mat_delete_expression.description: Delete a material expression node by its ID.

mat_disconnect.description: Disconnect an input pin on an expression node.

mat_get_info.description: Get material info: settings and list of expression nodes.

mat_list_expressions.description: List all expression nodes in a material with class, name, position, and ID.

mat_list_pins.description:
List ALL input and output pins of a material expression node.
        Essential for discovering Substrate BSDF pin names.

mat_set_expression_value.description:
Set a value on a material expression node.
        Constant: property_name='R', value=1.52
        Constant3Vector: property_name='Constant', value='(R=0.8,G=0.9,B=0.95,A=1.0)'
        Fresnel: 'Expon

mat_set_property.description: Set a material property: blend_mode, shading_model, two_sided, translucency_lighting_mode.

rc_batch.description:
Execute multiple Remote Control operations in a single transaction.
        Each request dict should have: objectPath, propertyName (for property ops)
        or objectPath, functionName, parameters (

rc_call_function.description:
Call any UFunction on a UObject via Remote Control API.
        object_path: path to the object. function_name: name of the UFUNCTION.
        parameters: dict of function parameters.

rc_get_preset.description: Get details of a specific Remote Control preset including its exposed properties and functions.

rc_get_property.description:
Read a property from any UObject via Unreal Remote Control API.
        object_path: full path like '/Game/Maps/Level.Level:PersistentLevel.MyActor.RootComponent'
        property_name: e.g. 'Relative

rc_list_presets.description:
List all Remote Control presets defined in the project.
        Presets expose specific properties/functions for remote control.

rc_search_objects.description:
Search for assets/objects in the Unreal project.
        query: search text. class_name: filter by class (e.g. 'StaticMesh').
        outer_path: filter by package path.

rc_set_property.description:
Write a property on any UObject via Unreal Remote Control API.
        value can be a number, string, boolean, or dict for struct types.

read_blueprint_content.description:
Read and analyze the complete content of a Blueprint including event graph, 
    functions, variables, components, and implemented interfaces.
    
    Args:
        blueprint_path: Full path to

rename_function.description:
Rename a function in a Blueprint.

    Args:
        blueprint_name: Name of the Blueprint to modify
        old_function_name: Current name of the function
        new_function_name: New name fo

set_actor_transform.description: Set the transform of an actor.

set_blueprint_variable_properties.description:
Modify properties of an existing Blueprint variable without deleting it.

    Preserves all VariableGet and VariableSet nodes connected to this variable.

    Args:
        blueprint_name: Name o

set_mesh_material_color.description: Set material color on a mesh component using the proven color system.

set_node_property.description:
Set a property on a Blueprint node or perform semantic node editing.

    This function supports both simple property modifications and advanced semantic
    node editing operations (pin manageme

set_physics_properties.description: Set physics properties on a component.

set_pin_value.description:
Set a default value on a specific pin of a Blueprint node.
    node_id: the node GUID or name (e.g. 'K2Node_CallFunction_0').
    pin_name: the pin to set (e.g. 'Radius', 'Height', 'RadialSteps').

set_static_mesh_properties.description: Set static mesh properties on a StaticMeshComponent.

spawn_physics_blueprint_actor.description:
Quickly spawn a single actor with physics, color, and a specific mesh.

    This is the primary function for creating simple objects with physics properties.
    It handles creating a temporary B

tpl_gs_column.description:
Create a classical column with base, shaft, and capital.
        Uses cylinder primitives composed together.

tpl_gs_cup.description:
Create a complete hollow cup mesh using boolean subtract.
        Creates outer cylinder, inner cavity cylinder, boolean subtracts, and optionally spawns.

tpl_gs_pipe.description:
Create a pipe (hollow tube) along a path using sweep polygon.
        path_points: list of [x,y,z] waypoints for the pipe centerline.

tpl_gs_terrain.description:
Create a procedural terrain patch using a subdivided box + Perlin noise displacement.
        size: terrain size in XY. subdivisions: detail level. noise_magnitude/frequency: terrain variation.

tpl_mat_emissive.description:
Create an emissive material. Great for glowing objects, lights, and UI elements.
        color: [R,G,B] emissive color. intensity: multiplier for brightness.

tpl_mat_glass.description:
Create a complete Substrate glass material with SlabBSDF, TransmittanceToMFP,
        Fresnel refraction, and all nodes connected. Ready to use.
        tint_color: [R,G,B] for glass color tint (white

tpl_mat_pbr.description:
Create a complete PBR material with Substrate SlabBSDF. Ready to use.
        base_color: [R,G,B] diffuse color. roughness: 0=mirror, 1=rough. metallic: 0=dielectric, 1=metal.

tpl_scene_arrange_circle.description:
Arrange Blueprint actors in a circle pattern.
        face_center: if True, actors rotate to face the center point.

tpl_scene_arrange_grid.description:
Spawn a grid of Blueprint actors. Great for testing materials or layouts.
        Creates rows*cols actors evenly spaced.

tpl_scene_create_and_place.description:
Spawn a Blueprint actor and optionally apply a material - all in one call.
        blueprint_path: e.g. '/Game/Blueprints/BP_GS_Cup'
        material_path: optional material to apply (e.g. '/Game/Geom
